steps.title: Trace the purpose
steps.content: The passage first says Spanish-language newspapers flourished across Texas in the late 1800s, then gives San Antonio as an example. It shifts to El Paso as the strongest case, noting its twenty-two Spanish-language newspapers and explaining why the city was such a rich site for journalism.

steps.title: Rule out extra claims
steps.content: A adds a present-day comparison the passage never makes. C invents an influence from El Paso to San Antonio. D turns Mexico into the audience for Texas newspapers, but Mexico is mentioned only to explain El Paso's location and Spanish-speaking population. The answer is B.
